name: Short Rest
description: A Short Rest is a period of downtime, at least 1 hour long, during which a character does nothing more strenuous than eating, drinking, reading, and tending to wounds.
effects: Lasts at least 1 hour
A character can spend one or more Hit Dice at the end of a Short Rest to regain Hit Points
For each Hit Die spent, roll the die and add the character's Constitution modifier; the character regains that many HP (minimum 0)
The character can decide to spend additional Hit Dice after each roll
Some class features recharge on a Short Rest (e.g., Warlock spell slots, Fighter's Second Wind, Channel Divinity)

name: Long Rest
description: A Long Rest is an extended period of downtime, at least 8 hours long, during which a character sleeps for at least 6 hours and performs no more than 2 hours of light activity such as reading, talking, eating, or standing watch.
effects: Lasts at least 8 hours, with at least 6 hours of sleep and up to 2 hours of light activity
At the end of a Long Rest, the character regains all lost Hit Points
The character regains spent Hit Dice, up to a number equal to half their total Hit Dice (minimum of 1 die)
All expended spell slots are restored
One level of Exhaustion is removed (if the character has eaten and drunk water)
A character can't benefit from more than one Long Rest in a 24-hour period
If the rest is interrupted by a period of strenuous activity (at least 1 hour of walking, fighting, casting spells, or similar), the characters must begin the rest again

name: Exhaustion
description: Some special abilities and environmental hazards, such as starvation and the long-term effects of freezing or scorching temperatures, can lead to a special condition called Exhaustion. Exhaustion is measured in levels, and each level imposes increasing penalties.
effects: Each level of Exhaustion applies a cumulative -2 penalty to all d20 rolls (ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws)
Level 1: -2 to d20 rolls
Level 2: -4 to d20 rolls
Level 3: -6 to d20 rolls
Level 4: -8 to d20 rolls
Level 5: -10 to d20 rolls
Level 6: Speed reduced to 0
Level 10: Death
Finishing a Long Rest reduces Exhaustion by 1 level (if the creature has eaten and drunk water)
Greater Restoration spell reduces Exhaustion by 1 level

name: Hit Dice Recovery
description: Hit Dice are a resource used to regain Hit Points during Short Rests. You recover spent Hit Dice at the end of a Long Rest.
effects: Each class determines your Hit Die type (e.g., d6 for Sorcerer, d8 for Bard, d10 for Fighter, d12 for Barbarian)
Your total number of Hit Dice equals your character level
During a Short Rest, you can spend Hit Dice to regain HP: roll the die + Constitution modifier
At the end of a Long Rest, you regain spent Hit Dice equal to half your total number of Hit Dice (minimum of 1)
You choose which Hit Dice to spend (relevant for multiclass characters with different Hit Die sizes)

name: Food and Water
description: Characters who don't eat or drink suffer the effects of exhaustion. A character needs one pound of food per day and one gallon of water per day (or two gallons in hot weather).
effects: A character needs 1 pound of food per day
A character needs 1 gallon of water per day (2 gallons in hot weather)
A character can go without food for a number of days equal to 3 + Constitution modifier (minimum 1) before suffering Exhaustion; each day beyond that adds 1 level of Exhaustion
A character with access to less water than required must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or gain 1 level of Exhaustion (or 2 levels if already Exhausted)
Characters who eat or drink half the required amount can survive at half the normal duration before Exhaustion sets in

name: Interrupting a Rest
description: If a rest is interrupted by strenuous activity, the characters must begin the rest again to gain any benefit from it.
effects: Short Rest: Interrupted by any strenuous activity such as fighting, casting spells, or at least 10 minutes of walking
Long Rest: Interrupted if the characters engage in at least 1 hour of strenuous activity (walking, fighting, casting spells, or similar)
A brief interruption (e.g., a single combat encounter lasting a few rounds) during a Long Rest does not necessarily require restarting, provided the activity takes less than 1 hour
Standing watch during a Long Rest counts as light activity and does not interrupt the rest
